text.title: Agent Workflow, Tool Ecosystem, and Gotchas
text.summary: How agents interact with design system tooling, key services, and critical failure modes to avoid.

list.item: storybook CLI — build and serve component documentation; npx storybook init / storybook.js.org
style-dictionary CLI — transform design tokens to platform-specific formats; npm install -g style-dictionary
chromatic CLI — visual regression testing for design system components; npm install -g chromatic
ts-morph — TypeScript AST parser for prop extraction from component source; github.com/dsherret/ts-morph
figma-export — export Figma design tokens to JSON/CSS; npm package
Figma REST API (SaaS) — fetch component properties, variables, published library metadata; agent-friendly
Storybook (OSS) — agent can write story files and trigger builds via Node.js API
Chromatic (SaaS) — visual diff on component changes; CLI + API for CI integration
Supernova (SaaS) — token sync Figma to code; design system governance; REST API
Tokens Studio (Figma plugin) — token management with GitHub sync; agent can read exported JSON

section.title: Agent Workflow

rule.statement: An agent can parse component source (React/Vue/Angular), extract prop APIs, generate Storybook stories or MDX docs, and diff them against Figma component properties via the Figma REST API. Variant generation requires a style-guide prompt constraining the output to known token values.
rule.rationale: Unconstrained variant generation produces components that use arbitrary values. The token constraint is the design system's authority — the agent must operate within it.

section.reference: Agent prompt: generate Storybook stories and MDX docs from component source
Agent prompt: audit component library for token usage violations

antipattern.description: Agent misrepresents union types and complex generics when parsing TypeScript prop interfaces
antipattern.reason: ts-morph or AST parsing handles complex types accurately; LLM-only prop extraction from code text makes errors on union, intersection, and conditional types.

antipattern.description: Agent reads Figma API component properties and treats them as current without checking the timestamp
antipattern.reason: Figma API returns data as it exists in Figma, which may lag code by weeks. Always timestamp both sources and surface the lag in the diff report.

antipattern.description: Agent triggers synchronous Chromatic visual regression runs and blocks on them
antipattern.reason: Visual regression on a component library can take 10+ minutes. Agent-triggered Chromatic runs must be async with webhook callback, not inline in a synchronous pipeline step.

antipattern.description: Agent generating token suggestions based on Material Design or Ant Design defaults
antipattern.reason: Token suggestion models are trained on generic design systems. Suggestions may not fit brand personality or existing token naming conventions. Always review against actual brand guidelines.
